key_insight: The paper formalizes core q-series machinery in Lean and uses it to machine-check classic results like the Jacobi Triple Product and the Rogers-Ramanujan identities.
engineering_relevance: It provides reusable proof infrastructure for scalable theorem proving in number theory, combinatorics, and future formalization of modular and mock modular objects.
method: Build formal definitions for q-Pochhammer symbols and q-binomial coefficients.
Develop foundational lemmas such as Bailey's Lemma in Lean.
Use the framework to verify landmark identities end-to-end.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports fully verified proofs of the Jacobi Triple Product formula and the Rogers-Ramanujan identities as benchmark validations.
watch_item: Formalizing q-series must carefully reconcile algebraic identities with analytic convergence, which can make the library design delicate and hard to scale.
paper_title: Formalized $q$-series: The Rogers-Ramanujan Identities and Beyond
